title: 6H: browser smoke harness for isolated prototypes
goal: Add a standalone browser smoke harness for the Exercise Lab that can enumerate discovered prototype records, load each runner in system Chrome, detect page/console failures and unexpected network or storage use, and exercise a minimal keyboard focus path. Keep it generic and verification-only; do not create a shared exercise runtime or alter classroom code.
acceptance: The lab exposes an npm run test:smoke command using a locked browser-test dependency and system Chrome or a documented equivalent, without changing existing validate/check/build semantics.
The harness discovers prototype IDs from the catalog or a generated manifest rather than hardcoding the initial EX-0001-only catalog, so later merged 6H records are automatically included.

For each selected prototype it loads /runner/?id=EX-NNNN, waits for the isolated component to mount, fails on runner load errors or uncaught console/page errors, and confirms at least one enabled focusable pupil interaction exists.
The harness fails on unexpected external network requests and checks that prototypes do not write localStorage or sessionStorage during initial load.
An environment filter allows a review slice to smoke only its three exercise IDs, while domain/final gates can run the complete discovered set.
The existing EX-0001 prototype passes the harness, catalog validation, strict check, and production build.
must_ask_if: The pinned decision, research, production-plan, or slice-graph inputs in experiments/design/6h-batch-inputs.json do not match the working baseline.
Installing the browser-test dependency or using system Chrome is unavailable, conflicts with repository package policy, or requires credentials/permissions.
A reliable smoke harness would require changing the Exercise Lab runtime, catalog schema, an existing exercise, classroom code, or files outside declared areas.
The proposed harness would send data externally or persist pupil/test state.
